questions.q: What was the real birth name of the Bengali matinee idol Uttam Kumar?
questions.explanation: Uttam Kumar was born Arun Kumar Chatterjee in 1926 and later adopted the screen name Uttam Kumar.

questions.q: By what honorific title is the actor Uttam Kumar popularly remembered in Bengali cinema?
questions.explanation: Uttam Kumar is popularly titled 'Mahanayak', meaning 'Great Hero', in Bengali cinema.

questions.q: Which actress formed the most celebrated romantic screen pairing with Uttam Kumar in Bengali cinema?
questions.explanation: The pairing of Uttam Kumar and Suchitra Sen defined commercial Bengali romance through the 1950s and 1960s.

questions.q: Uttam Kumar was the leading star of which regional film industry of India?
questions.explanation: Uttam Kumar was the foremost star of Bengali cinema, the industry based in Tollygunge, Kolkata, known as Tollywood.

questions.q: In which Indian city was the Bengali film industry of Uttam Kumar primarily based?
questions.explanation: Uttam Kumar's Bengali film industry was centred in the Tollygunge area of Kolkata.

questions.q: In which 1954 film did the Uttam Kumar–Suchitra Sen pairing achieve a major breakthrough success?
questions.explanation: Agni Pariksha (1954) was a landmark hit that cemented the Uttam Kumar–Suchitra Sen romantic partnership.

questions.q: Which acclaimed Bengali director cast Uttam Kumar in the 1966 film Nayak?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray directed Uttam Kumar in Nayak (1966), a film he wrote specifically with the star in mind.

questions.q: In which decade did Uttam Kumar pass away?
questions.explanation: Uttam Kumar died in 1980, at the age of 53.

questions.q: The primary language of Uttam Kumar's most famous films was which of the following?
questions.explanation: Uttam Kumar starred chiefly in Bengali-language films, though he also appeared in a few Hindi productions.

questions.q: In which 1961 romantic film did Uttam Kumar and Suchitra Sen star together, famous for its 'Ei Poth Jodi Na Sesh Hoy' song?
questions.explanation: Saptapadi (1961) paired Uttam Kumar and Suchitra Sen and featured the celebrated duet 'Ei Poth Jodi Na Sesh Hoy'.

questions.q: In Satyajit Ray's Nayak (1966), what was the profession of the character played by Uttam Kumar?
questions.explanation: In Nayak, Uttam Kumar plays Arindam Mukherjee, a celebrated matinee idol travelling by train, mirroring his own stardom.

questions.q: In which year was Uttam Kumar born?
questions.explanation: Uttam Kumar was born on 3 September 1926 in Kolkata.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray followed Nayak by casting Uttam Kumar in which 1967 detective-style film?
questions.explanation: Ray cast Uttam Kumar as detective Byomkesh Bakshi in Chiriyakhana (1967).

questions.q: Before becoming an actor, Uttam Kumar reportedly worked as a clerk at which organisation?
questions.explanation: Uttam Kumar worked as a clerk at the Calcutta Port Commissioners' office before establishing his film career.

questions.q: Which 1957 Ajoy Kar film starring Uttam Kumar and Suchitra Sen revolves around a man who loses his memory in an accident?
questions.explanation: Harano Sur (1957), directed by Ajoy Kar and produced by Uttam Kumar, paired him with Suchitra Sen in a celebrated amnesia romance.

questions.q: Uttam Kumar shared a long professional partnership in his later years with which actress, also his companion?
questions.explanation: Uttam Kumar's long association with actress Supriya Devi included films such as Sonar Harin and Chowringhee.

questions.q: In which 1968 film did Uttam Kumar play a hotel receptionist named Shankar at the fictional Shahjahan Hotel?
questions.explanation: In Chowringhee (1968), Uttam Kumar played Shankar, a receptionist at the grand Shahjahan Hotel.

questions.q: Uttam Kumar starred in the 1967 film Antony Firingee, based on the life of a Bengali poet-singer of which heritage?
questions.explanation: Antony Firingee depicts Anthony Firinghee, a Portuguese-origin man who became a noted Bengali kabigan poet-singer.

questions.q: Uttam Kumar produced and starred in which 1967 Hindi film that paired him with Vyjayanthimala and became a costly box-office failure?
questions.explanation: Chhoti Si Mulaqat (1967), produced by and starring Uttam Kumar with Vyjayanthimala, was a costly flop that reportedly left him in heavy financial difficulty.

questions.q: The 1975 bilingual hit Amanush, made in both Bengali and Hindi, paired Uttam Kumar with which actress?
questions.explanation: Amanush (1975) paired Uttam Kumar with Sharmila Tagore and became a major bilingual success.

questions.q: Which music director composed the popular songs of the Uttam Kumar–Sharmila Tagore film Amanush (1975)?
questions.explanation: Shyamal Mitra composed and sang for Amanush, including its hit Hindi number 'Dil Aisa Kisi Ne Mera Toda'.

questions.q: The famous playback voice most associated with songs picturised on Uttam Kumar belonged to which singer?
questions.explanation: Hemanta Mukherjee (Hemant Kumar) was the playback voice most closely identified with Uttam Kumar's on-screen songs.

questions.q: Uttam Kumar's career is often said to have struggled at first, with his early films flopping; which 1953 film is generally regarded as his first big hit?
questions.explanation: Sare Chuattor (1953), a comedy also notable as Suchitra Sen's debut, gave Uttam Kumar his first major box-office success.

questions.q: Uttam Kumar won a National Film Award (Best Actor) in the inaugural year of that acting category for which two films?
questions.explanation: In 1967 Uttam Kumar became the first winner of the National Film Award for Best Actor, recognised for Antony Firingee and Chiriyakhana.

questions.q: In which year did Uttam Kumar die after suffering a heart attack?
questions.explanation: Uttam Kumar died on 24 July 1980 in Kolkata following a heart attack.

questions.q: The 1964 film Jotugriha, exploring a disintegrating marriage, paired Uttam Kumar with which actress?
questions.explanation: Jotugriha (1964) paired Uttam Kumar with Suchitra Sen in a sombre portrait of a failing marriage.

questions.q: In Satyajit Ray's Nayak (1966), which actress played the magazine editor who interviews Uttam Kumar's character on the train?
questions.explanation: Sharmila Tagore played Aditi, the editor who interviews Arindam, in Ray's Nayak.

questions.q: Uttam Kumar made his film debut in the late 1940s; under which name was he initially credited in his very first films?
questions.explanation: In his earliest films, including his 1948 debut Drishtidaan, he was credited as Arun Kumar before adopting Uttam Kumar.

questions.q: Uttam Kumar played an idealistic, patriotic doctor in which acclaimed 1975 film, sometimes cited among his finest later performances?
questions.explanation: In Agnishwar (1975), Uttam Kumar gave a celebrated dramatic performance as the idealistic doctor Agnishwar Mukhopadhyay.

questions.q: A landmark metro station in Kolkata was renamed in honour of Uttam Kumar; which station bears his name?
questions.explanation: Kolkata's Tollygunge Metro station was renamed Mahanayak Uttam Kumar in his honour.
